catch_key.problem: Broken object-level access control (IDOR / OWASP A01): the GetRelease read path (and the release-attachment handlers, and the ListReleases drafts flag) returns DRAFT (unpublished) releases to any caller with mere repo READ access — on a public repo, even anonymously. The repo-scoped fetch and the IsTag check make the path LOOK authorized, but a draft is unpublished content that needs WRITE-level permission.
catch_key.correct_direction: ADD a stronger object-level authorization gate for draft releases — require the caller to have write/maintainer access to releases (signed in AND CanWrite on the releases unit) before returning a draft, and apply it to GetRelease, the attachment handlers, and the include-drafts listing. The repo read gate is necessary but NOT sufficient for drafts.
catch_key.inverted_direction: Concluding the existing repo-scoped fetch / IsTag check already authorizes the read and that draft visibility is fine, OR recommending to RELAX access (e.g. expose drafts more broadly, drop a check) — i.e. affirming the vulnerable behavior as correct instead of tightening it.

file: the Gitea releases REST API, EXACTLY the 2 files the commit modified, in full and uncurated: routers/api/v1/repo/release.go (421L — GetRelease/GetLatestRelease/ListReleases/CreateRelease/EditRelease/DeleteRelease) + routers/api/v1/repo/release_attachment.go (407L — the release-attachment GET/list/CRUD handlers). 832 lines total before the fix.
fix_summary: BROKEN ACCESS CONTROL / IDOR (OWASP A01) — a missing OBJECT-LEVEL authorization check, the aegis lesson 'enforce server-side authorization on every read; a partial check is not the check'. GetRelease fetches a release with repo_model.GetReleaseForRepoByID(ctx.Repo.Repository.ID, id) and returns it after only a repo-match and an IsTag check — but it does NOT gate DRAFT (unpublished) releases, so any caller with mere repo READ access (and, on a public repo, an anonymous user) can fetch an unpublished draft release by id, plus its attachments (ListReleaseAttachments / GetReleaseAttachment via checkReleaseMatchRepo) and see drafts in ListReleases. Draft releases are unpublished content that should require WRITE access. The maintainer's fix.diff adds canAccessDraftRelease(ctx) (signed in AND CanWrite(unit.TypeReleases) AND the API token carries repo-write scope) and gates GetRelease, the attachment handlers, and the IncludeDrafts flag of ListReleases behind it. THE ATTRACTOR (why this discriminates): there IS already an access control here — the release is fetched scoped to the current repo (GetReleaseForRepoByID) and there is an IsTag 404 — so the read path LOOKS authorized; the missing nuance (a draft is a sub-resource that needs a STRONGER permission than the repo's read gate) is Gitea-specific domain knowledge a reviewer glosses over. The repo-scoped fetch is the present-but-incomplete control that misdirects, structurally like 'parameterized != authorized'. SCOPE: before.txt is the WHOLE 2 files the commit touched, full and UN-curated — the missing draft gate is determinable from context but buried in ~832 lines of idiomatic API code, NOT excerpted to point at it. DISCRIMINATION EVIDENCE (pre-validated before adoption): a blind, security-primed no-skill review of before.txt MISSED this entirely — its top finding was a speculative nil-pointer/operator-precedence DoS hazard it itself judged 'safe in that path', plus a CreateRelease logic nitpick and an attachment check it concluded was 'actually sound'; it never once reasoned about draft-release visibility. So the base arm chases a hedged distractor while leaving the real, confirmed access-control hole un-flagged. Unlike the prior kubestellar fixture (a vuln buffet where the base arm found 5+ other confirmed vulns and got capped at 8), this file is otherwise clean — the draft access-control issue is the DOMINANT confirmed security problem, so missing it should actually cost score. PROVENANCE: a real, reviewed, merged Gitea PR (#36659) authored by Lunny Xiao (Gitea's founder); Copilot is a co-author (drafting assistance only) — the project's own accepted change. The grader scores the review against fix.diff and does not see this note. Repo: go-gitea/gitea, MIT, ~56k stars.
